prompt:
Theorem title: Full Glassey blow-up proof

Ambient theorem statement (context only; do not formalize this whole statement unless it exactly matches the target node):
Glassey blow-up argument for the focusing mass-critical nonlinear Schrodinger equation.

Consider a sufficiently regular solution \(u(t,x)\) to the focusing mass-critical NLS on \(\mathbb{R}^d\):

\[
i\partial_t u+\Delta u=-|u|^{4/d}u.
\]

Assume:

- \(u_0\in H^1(\mathbb{R}^d)\),

- \(|x|u_0\in L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)\),

- the energy \(E(u_0)\) is negative, where

\[
E(u)=\frac12\int_{\mathbb{R}^d}|\nabla u|^2\,dx
-\frac{1}{2+4/d}\int_{\mathbb{R}^d}|u|^{2+4/d}\,dx.
\]

Then the maximal forward existence time \(T\) is finite and

\[
\|u(t)\|_{H^1(\mathbb{R}^d)}\to\infty
\quad\text{as }t\uparrow T.
\]

Primary formalization target: the target node's informal statement and informal proof text below. Do not try to prove the ambient theorem statement itself unless it is identical to the target node.

Target theorem name: weighted_l2_ineq_aristotle

Scratch file to rewrite: FormalIslands/Generated/weighted_l2_ineq_worker_20260405-235300_144c5049.lean

Target node:

- id: weighted_l2_ineq
- title: Weighted L2 Inequality
- informal statement:
For sufficiently regular \(f:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{C}\), \(\|f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}^2\le\frac{2}{d}\,\|xf\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\,\|\nabla f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\).
- informal proof text:
Write \(\|f\|_{L^2}^2=\int|f|^2\,dx=\frac{1}{d}\int(\operatorname{div}x)|f|^2\,dx=-\frac{1}{d}\int x\cdot\nabla(|f|^2)\,dx=-\frac{2}{d}\operatorname{Re}\int x\cdot\nabla f\,\overline{f}\,dx\). Applying \(|\operatorname{Re}z|\le|z|\) and Cauchy-Schwarz: \(\|f\|_{L^2}^2\le\frac{2}{d}\left|\int x\cdot\nabla f\,\overline{f}\,dx\right|\le\frac{2}{d}\,\|xf\|_{L^2}\,\|\nabla f\|_{L^2}\).
- formalization priority: 2
- formalization rationale:
The weighted L2 inequality is a self-contained, reusable lemma with a clean one-integration-by-parts proof via div(x)=d and Cauchy-Schwarz. Compact to formalize and independently useful beyond this theorem.

Coverage sketch:

- summary: For sufficiently regular \(f:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{C}\), \(\|f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}^2\le\frac{2}{d}\,\|xf\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\,\|\nabla f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\).

  - [setup] Write \(\|f\|_{L^2}^2=\int|f|^2\,dx=\frac{1}{d}\int(\operatorname{div}x)|f|^2\,dx=-\frac{1}{d}\int x\cdot\nabla(|f|^2)\,dx=-\frac{2}{d}\operatorname{Re}\int x\cdot\nabla f\,\overline{f}\,dx\).
  - [local_inference] Applying \(|\operatorname{Re}z|\le|z|\) and Cauchy-Schwarz: \(\|f\|_{L^2}^2\le\frac{2}{d}\left|\int x\cdot\nabla f\,\overline{f}\,dx\right|\le\frac{2}{d}\,\|xf\|_{L^2}\,\|\nabla f\|_{L^2}\).

- id: root
  title: Full Glassey Blow-up Theorem
  informal statement: Under the hypotheses \(u_0\in H^1\), \(|x|u_0\in L^2\), and \(E(u_0)<0\), the focusing mass-critical NLS solution blows up in finite forward time with \(\|u(t)\|_{H^1}\to\infty\) as \(t\uparrow T<\infty\).
- id: virial_identity
  title: Virial Identity
  informal statement: For any sufficiently regular solution of the focusing mass-critical NLS, the variance \(V(t)=\int_{\mathbb{R}^d}|x|^2|u(t,x)|^2\,dx\) satisfies \(V''(t)=8\int|\nabla u|^2\,dx-\frac{16}{2+4/d}\int|u|^{2+4/d}\,dx=16E(u(t))\).
